title: Lesson 30
paragraph: This lesson focuses on practical B1 vocabulary for everyday communication.

words.meaning: to give someone hope or support
words.example: Teachers encourage students to speak in English.
Many kinds of plants exist in this forest.

words.meaning: to bend paper, cloth, or another flat thing
words.example: Fold the paper in half before cutting it.

words.meaning: to show someone where to go or what to do
words.example: The teacher will guide us through the museum.

words.meaning: to move or do something quickly
words.example: We need to hurry or we will miss the train.
Students tried to invent a useful kitchen tool.

words.meaning: to ask someone to come to an event or place
words.example: We invite our neighbors to dinner every summer.

words.meaning: to hit a door or surface to get attention
words.example: Please knock before you enter the room.

words.meaning: to give something to someone for a short time
words.example: Can you lend me your pen for a minute?

words.meaning: to move something to a higher position
words.example: Do not lift the heavy box alone.
